title: The Great Vigil of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 21, 2019 | "The Great Vigil of Easter" | Preacher: Richard Lawson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on Easter Sunday, April 21, 2019, the principal feast of the Christian year celebrating the Resurrection of Jesus Christ. The Great Vigil of Easter (typically held Holy Saturday evening into Easter morning) is the longest and most elaborate liturgy in the Anglican tradition, featuring multiple Old Testament readings culminating in the Easter proclamation and renewal of baptismal vows. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the primary texts would have included readings from Genesis (the creation account), Exodus (the parting of the Red Sea), Isaiah (salvation oracles), and culminating in the Gospel account of the Resurrection (likely from Matthew's Gospel in Year A). The church was in the Easter Season, having just completed Lent and Holy Week.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative institutional stability in April 2019, between the 79th General Convention (2018) and the upcoming 80th Convention (2021). Bishop Robert Wright had been leading the Diocese of Colorado since 2013 and would remain in place through this period. The broader Anglican Communion was navigating ongoing tensions around human sexuality and biblical interpretation, but these were not acutely heightened at this specific moment. Saint John's Cathedral itself, as the historic seat of the Diocese of Colorado (established in the late 19th century), would have been preparing for significant Easter observances.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in late April 2019 was experiencing spring weather with above-average temperatures and below-average precipitation—the city had been in moderate drought conditions throughout early 2019. The neighborhood around downtown Denver and the Cathedral area was experiencing continued gentrification and development pressures. Colorado's legislature was in its final weeks of the 2019 session (adjourned May 8), debating various bills on education funding, healthcare, and other issues. No major crises or disasters directly affected the Denver metropolitan area in the week prior to Easter.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Mueller Report on Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election had been released on April 18, 2019 (just three days before this sermon), dominating national news and creating significant political controversy over its conclusions regarding obstruction of justice. The Trump administration was in its third year, with immigration and border security remaining contentious political issues. The college admissions bribery scandal (Operation Varsity Blues) was in active prosecution phase, with major revelations and celebrity indictments occurring in late March and early April. The economy was performing well by standard metrics, though political polarization remained extremely high.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Notre-Dame de Reims Cathedral in Paris suffered a catastrophic fire on April 15, 2019 (six days before this sermon), with the devastating loss of the roof and spire being a major international news story still dominating coverage on Easter Sunday. The Brexit crisis continued to grip British politics as the UK parliament remained deadlocked over withdrawal terms from the European Union. Venezuela was experiencing severe humanitarian crisis under Nicolas Maduro, with ongoing humanitarian interventions and geopolitical tension involving the United States.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The cultural moment of April 2019 was characterized by political exhaustion and deep partisan division in the United States, with the Mueller Report release renewing intense debate about the 2016 election and Trump's presidency. The Easter season offered religious Americans a traditional moment of spiritual renewal and gathering, potentially providing respite from the surrounding political tumult. In popular culture, Game of Thrones was in its final season (the series finale aired May 19, 2019), and the Marvel Cinematic Universe's Avengers: Endgame had recently broken box office records (released April 26, 2019—five days after this sermon, so advance anticipation was high). For Denver's Catholic and Protestant communities, the Notre-Dame fire was particularly resonant given the symbolic importance of cathedral spaces to Christian identity.
